examples: Centromere is the primary constriction on the chromosome, a region at which the sister chromatids are held together.
Following the replication of chromosomes resultant chromatids remain attached at the centromere.
The chromosomes were measured and analysed on the basis of their relative length and centromere index.
That is taxing because some parts of a chromosome, such as those near the centromere (the place where the two arms of a chromosome meet) are not easy to get at.
